title: Final Exam Practice: All Topics of Kazakhstan History
overview: This topic consolidates the key facts, dates, and concepts tested in the course final exam. It covers all 10 lecture topics: prehistory and the Saka period, the Turkic Khaganate, Ulug Ulus and Kazakh Khanate, Russian colonization, national liberation uprisings, the Alash movement, Soviet modernization, Soviet reforms and Perestroika, independence, and foreign policy. Questions are drawn directly from the actual final exam format — mixed MCQ, true/false, and fill-in-the-blank — to simulate exam conditions.

coreConcepts.definition: First Turkic state, founded by Bumin and Mukan Khagans after defeating the Rouran. Split into Western and Eastern Khaganates in 603.
coreConcepts.example: She-wolf origin legend recorded in Chinese chronicles (Sui Shu).

coreConcepts.definition: Eneolithic culture of northern Kazakhstan providing the earliest archaeological evidence of horse domestication.
coreConcepts.example: Dental wear from bits and lipid residues of mare's milk found at Botai sites.

coreConcepts.definition: Western ulus of the Mongol Empire, known in Turkic sources as 'Uluq Ulys' (Great Ulus). Flourished under Uzbek Khan and his successor Janibek.
coreConcepts.example: Correct Turkic name for the state usually called the 'Golden Horde' in Russian sources.

coreConcepts.definition: Kazakh national-democratic party founded July 1917; its government Alash-Orda proclaimed December 1917 under chairman Alikhan Bukeikhanov.
coreConcepts.example: Karkaraly petition (June 1905) → beginning of the Alash movement.

coreConcepts.definition: Unified Kazakh legal code created by Tauke Khan together with the three great biys (Tole Bi, Kazybek Bi, Aiteke Bi) to unify the three Zhuz against the Dzungars.
coreConcepts.example: NOT to be confused with Qasym Khan's code ('Qasym Khannyn Qasqa Zholy').

coreConcepts.definition: Forced Soviet collectivization that sedentarized Kazakh nomads and confiscated livestock, causing famine killing ~40% of Kazakhs (Asharshylyk).
coreConcepts.example: Kazakh population fell from ~4 million to ~2.5 million; intelligentsia wrote the 'Letter of Five' in protest.

coreConcepts.definition: Declaration of State Sovereignty of Kazakh SSR adopted 25 October 1990. Independence declared 16 December 1991. Tenge introduced 15 November 1993.
coreConcepts.example: Goals: national self-governance + independent international relations.

coreConcepts.definition: Article identifying 7 symbols of Kazakh steppe civilization, including the Golden Man, Ancient Turkic Writing, and the Great Silk Road.
coreConcepts.example: Used in Kazakhstan's national branding and heritage education.

expandedNotes.heading: Key Dates to Memorize
expandedNotes.body: 552 – Turkic Khaganate founded. 1446 – Abulkhair Khan conquers Sozak, Syganak, Ak-Korgan. 1731 – Abulkhair Khan accepts Russian protectorate. 1801 – Bukeev (Inner) Horde formed between Volga and Ural. 1836–1838 – Isatai-Makhambet uprising in Bukey Horde. 1841 – Kenesary takes power, introduces military court. 1905 (June) – Karkaraly petition (beginning of Alash). 1916 – National liberation uprising against tsarist conscription. 1917 (July) – Alash Party founded at First All-Kazakh Congress. 1917 (December) – Alash Autonomy proclaimed at Second All-Kazakh Congress. 1920 (26 August) – KazASSR formed, capital Orenburg. 1949 – First Soviet nuclear test at Semipalatinsk. 1952 – Bekmakhanov Affair. 1990 (25 October) – Declaration of State Sovereignty. 1991 (16 December) – Independence of Kazakhstan. 1993 (15 November) – Tenge introduced. 1995 – Rehabilitation of Dulatov, Zhumabaev, Kudaiberdiev.

expandedNotes.heading: Key People and Their Roles
expandedNotes.body: BUMIN & MUKAN – founding Khagans of Turkic Khaganate (552). ABULKHAIR KHAN – Khan of Younger Zhuz, organized resistance to Dzungars 1723-1730, accepted Russian protectorate 1731. ABLAI KHAN – defeated Dzungar warrior Sharysh; later Khan of all three Zhuz. TAUKE KHAN – created Zhety Zhargy code with three biys; sent embassies to Russia against Jungars. QASYM KHAN – expanded Khanate including Tashkent; diplomatic relations with Vasili III. SYRYM DATOV – led Junior Zhuz uprising 1783-1797 (Edil to Aral Sea). ISATAI TAIMANOV & MAKHAMBET UTEMISOV – led 1836-1838 Bukey Horde uprising. KENESARY KASYMOV – last Kazakh Khan, introduced military court in 1841. ALIKHAN BUKEIKHANOV – chairman of Alash-Orda; executed 1937, rehabilitated 1995. MIRZHAKYP DULATOV – poet 'Oyan Kazakh'; executed 1935, rehabilitated 1995. E. BEKMAKHANOV – Kazakh historian persecuted in 1952 Bekmakhanov Affair.

expandedNotes.heading: Soviet Period Key Events
expandedNotes.body: COLLECTIVIZATION (1929–1933): forced sedentarization; livestock confiscated; Great Famine (Asharshylyk) killed ~40% of Kazakhs. KazASSR formed 1920 (capital: Orenburg); renamed Kazakh SSR 1936 (capital: Alma-Ata). INDUSTRIALIZATION: Karaganda – main coal basin; Turksib railway built. STALINISM (1945–1953): Bekmakhanov Affair (1952); Semipalatinsk nuclear tests (from 1949); Kazakh Academy of Sciences created (1946). VIRGIN LANDS CAMPAIGN (from 1954): plowing of Kazakh steppes; wind erosion; Kazakh population reduced; major ecological damage. PERESTROIKA MOVEMENTS: Nevada-Semipalatinsk (anti-nuclear, Olzhas Suleimanov, 1989); Azat (independence movement); Jeltoqsan 1986 (protests after Kolbin's appointment). LATE USSR: Declaration of Sovereignty 1990; Independence 16 December 1991; tenge introduced 1993.

expandedNotes.heading: Cultural Heritage and Identity
expandedNotes.body: SAKA CULTURE: 'animal style' art; Golden Man (Esik barrow, ~4th c. BCE); earliest writing – silver bowl inscription; contributed to steppe democracy. BOTAI CULTURE: earliest horse domestication (3500-3000 BCE). TURKIC WRITING: Orkhon-Yenisei runic script; Kul Tegin and Bilge Khagan inscriptions (8th c.). GREAT SILK ROAD: Kazakhs at crossroads of trade; Syganak, Sozak, Turkestan as key cities. INDEPENDENCE-ERA FILMS: 'Nomad' (2005), 'Kurmanjan Datka' (2014), 'Tar Zaman' (2018) — historical films celebrating national identity. SOVIET CULTURE 1920s-30s: A. Zataevich (ethnomusicologist), K. Baiseitova (singer), A. Kashaubaev, S. Kozhamkulov (performers).

keyScholars.name: Bumin Khagan
keyScholars.contribution: Founded the Turkic Khaganate in 552 by defeating the Rouran; first Göktürk ruler.

keyScholars.name: Abulkhair Khan
keyScholars.contribution: Khan of Younger Zhuz who organized resistance to Dzungar invasions (1723-1730) and accepted Russian protectorate in 1731.

keyScholars.name: Tauke Khan
keyScholars.contribution: Created the unified legal code Zhety Zhargy together with the three great biys; unified the three Kazakh Zhuz.

keyScholars.name: Qasym Khan
keyScholars.contribution: Expanded the Kazakh Khanate to over 1 million km² including Tashkent; first Kazakhstan ruler known in Western Europe (per Herberstein).

keyScholars.name: Alikhan Bukeikhanov
keyScholars.contribution: Founder and chairman of Alash-Orda; leader of Kazakh national movement; executed 1937, rehabilitated 1995.

keyScholars.name: Mirzhakyp Dulatov
keyScholars.contribution: Poet and Alash activist, author of 'Oyan, Kazakh!' (1909); executed 1935, rehabilitated 1995.

keyScholars.name: Magzhan Zhumabaev
keyScholars.contribution: Poet and ideologue of Turkism within the Alash movement; executed 1938, rehabilitated 1995.

keyScholars.name: Yermukhan Bekmakhanov
keyScholars.contribution: Kazakh historian whose work on Kenesary was condemned in the 1952 Bekmakhanov Affair under Stalinist pressure.

keyScholars.name: Olzhas Suleimanov
keyScholars.contribution: Poet and public figure who founded the Nevada-Semipalatinsk anti-nuclear movement in 1989, successfully pushing for closure of Semipalatinsk test site.

keyScholars.name: Nursultan Nazarbayev
keyScholars.contribution: First President of Kazakhstan (1991–2019); signed the Declaration of Independence; introduced tenge (1993); authored '7 Facets of the Great Steppe'.

comparativeTables.title: Key Kazakh Uprisings Compared
rows: Land seizures, oppression in Bukey Horde

comparativeTables.title: Independence Milestones
rows: Established supremacy of Kazakh SSR laws; goals: self-governance + independent relations
National currency replacing Soviet ruble — economic sovereignty

timeline.event: Turkic Khaganate founded by Bumin and Mukan Khagans
Turkic Khaganate splits into Western and Eastern Khaganates
Mongol conquest; Kazakh steppe becomes Uluq Ulys (Golden Horde)
Kazakh Khanate founded by Janibek and Girey in the Zhetysu
Qasym Khan expands Khanate to 1M km², diplomatic ties with Moscow
Tauke Khan unifies three Zhuz; creates Zhety Zhargy legal code
Dzungar 'Great Disaster' (Aktaban Shubryndy) — Kazakhs forced to flee
Abulkhair Khan accepts Russian protectorate for Younger Zhuz
Bukeev (Inner) Horde formed between Volga and Ural
Kenesary Kasymov takes power; introduces military court
Karkaraly petition — beginning of Alash movement
National liberation uprising against tsarist conscription decree
Alash Party founded (July); Alash Autonomy proclaimed (December)
KazASSR established (26 August); capital Orenburg
Collectivization begins; Great Famine (Asharshylyk) 1930–1933
First Soviet nuclear test at Semipalatinsk Test Site
Bekmakhanov Affair — Stalinist persecution of Kazakh historian
Nevada-Semipalatinsk anti-nuclear movement founded by Suleimanov
Declaration of State Sovereignty of Kazakh SSR (25 October)
Independence of Kazakhstan declared (16 December)
Tenge introduced as national currency (15 November)
Rehabilitation of Alash leaders Dulatov, Zhumabaev, Kudaiberdiev

mustMemorize: Turkic Khaganate founded: 552 CE — Khagans: Bumin + Mukan
Bukeev Horde (1801) formed between VOLGA and URAL rivers
Zhety Zhargy = Tauke Khan (NOT Qasym Khan)
Alash Party = July 1917; Alash-Orda = December 1917; Chairman = Alikhan Bukeikhanov
Karkaraly petition June 1905 = beginning of Alash movement
Ablai defeated Dzungar warrior: SHARYSH
Abulkhair Khan organized anti-Dzungar resistance: 1723–1730
Botai culture = earliest horse domestication (3500–3000 BCE)
Golden Horde Turkic name: ULUQ ULYS
Sovereignty Declaration: 25 October 1990
Independence: 16 December 1991
Tenge introduced: 15 November 1993
KazASSR formed: 26 August 1920 (capital: Orenburg)
Rehabilitation of Dulatov, Zhumabaev, Kudaiberdiev: 1995
Kenesary in 1841 introduced: MILITARY court
Second All-Kazakh Congress named autonomy: ALASH
Isatai-Makhambet uprising (1836-1838): Bukey Horde, between Volga and Ural
Main Soviet coal center: KARAGANDA
Abulkhair Khan conquered in 1446: Sozak, Syganak, Ak-Korgan
Gorbachev foreign policy: withdrawal from Afghanistan + nuclear disarmament
cheatSheet: ANCIENT: Botai=horse domestication | Saka=animal style+Golden Man | 552=Turkic Khaganate(Bumin+Mukan) | 603=split | She-wolf→Turks. KHANATE: 1465=Kazakh Khanate | Qasym=expansion+Tashkent+VasiliIII | Tauke=Zhety Zhargy+biys | Uluq Ulys=Golden Horde. COLONIZATION: Abulkhair=resist Dzungars1723–30+Russian protectorate1731 | Ablai defeated SHARYSH | Bukeev Horde1801=Volga+Ural. UPRISINGS: Syrym=JuniorZhuz,Edil–Aral | Isatai-Makhambet=1836–38,BukeyHorde | Kenesary=1841,military court | 1916=conscription decree. ALASH: Karkaraly1905=start | Alash Party=July1917 | Alash-Orda=Dec1917=Bukeikhanov(chair) | KazASSR=26Aug1920,Orenburg | rehab1995. SOVIET: collectivization+famine1930–33 | Karaganda=coal | Bekmakhanov Affair1952 | Semipalatinsk tests1949. INDEPENDENCE: Sovereignty=25Oct1990 | Independence=16Dec1991 | Tenge=15Nov1993 | rehab Alash leaders=1995 | 7Facets=GoldenMan+TurkicWriting+SilkRoad.
